easy.title: Convert a Div-Based Layout to Semantic HTML
easy.description: Take a page built entirely with div elements and convert it to proper semantic HTML without changing the visual appearance. Replace all structural divs with appropriate elements (header, nav, main, article, section, aside, footer), replace interactive divs with buttons/links, and ensure proper heading hierarchy.

medium.title: Build a Semantic HTML Blog Template
medium.description: Build a complete blog template using only semantic HTML elements. The template must include: proper document structure (header, nav, main, footer), article listings with dates using <time>, comments section with <article> for each comment, pagination with <nav> and aria-label, search form with proper labels, sidebar with <aside>, and tags/categories using <ul>. No ARIA attributes allowed — rely solely on native HTML semantics.

hard.title: Refactor a Non-Semantic Web Application to Full Semantic HTML
hard.description: You are given a complete web application (e.g., a task/project management tool) built entirely with div elements and JavaScript event handlers. Refactor it to use proper semantic HTML throughout while maintaining all functionality. Requirements: replace all interactive divs with appropriate native elements (button, input, select, details/summary), restructure the layout with semantic landmarks, implement proper heading hierarchy, use lists for grouped items, add proper form labeling, and use the <dialog> element for modals. Remove all ARIA attributes that become unnecessary after using native HTML elements.

    Changes made: - div toolbar → header + menu - div task → li in ul - div add/filter → semantic buttons - div modal → HTMLDialogElement - div close × → button - All interactive divs replaced with buttons - Proper heading hierarchy (h1, h2, h3) - Form labels for inputs - aria-pressed for toggle buttons
